zh.Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Fra: 法國努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德維艾爾斯
zh.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres於1220年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德維艾爾斯。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1282年享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Veyres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Raymond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Raimund, of Germanic origin, from ragin ‘advice, decision’ + mund ‘protector’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. Subsequently it dropped out of use, but was revived in the middle of the 19th century, together with several other given names of Old English and Norman origin.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres was born in 1220, in Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1282, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres est né en 1220, à Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1282, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres wurde 1220 in Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1282 im Alter von 62 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres è nato il nel 1220 nel Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1282, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres(은)는 1220년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 지론드 베이레스에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1282년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres nasceu em 1220, em Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1282, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres родился в 1220 г. в местности веиресь, гиронд, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1282 г. в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Raymond IV de Veyres nació en 1220, en Vayres, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1282, a la edad de 62 años.
